Data Collection and Repository Metrics Analysis

# Import necessary libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(kableExtra)
library(Cairo)

# Set global ggplot theme - black axis lines, no grid
theme_set(theme_minimal() +
    theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")
    ))

Data Loading and Preprocessing

# Load the datasets
repos_df <- read_csv("../data/repos.csv")
metrics_df <- read_csv("../data/repo_metrics.csv")

# Process metrics_df - convert datetime and filter adoption time
metrics_df <- metrics_df %>%
    mutate(
        cursor_adoption_time = ymd_hms(cursor_adoption_time),
        adoption_yearmonth = format(cursor_adoption_time, "%Y-%m")
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(cursor_adoption_time)) %>%
    filter(cursor_adoption_time < ymd("2025-04-01")) %>%
    filter(cursor_adoption_time >= ymd("2024-01-01"))

# Get repos that have cursor adoption data in metrics_df
repos_with_adoption <- metrics_df %>%
    pull(repo_name) %>%
    unique()

# Convert datetime columns to R datetime format and filter to repos with cursor adoption
repos_df <- repos_df %>%
    filter(repo_name %in% repos_with_adoption) %>%
    mutate(
        repo_created = ymd_hms(repo_created),
        repo_updated = ymd_hms(repo_updated),
        repo_age_days = as.integer(difftime(Sys.Date(), repo_created, units = "days"))
    )

# View data structure
glimpse(repos_df)
## Rows: 806
## Columns: 14
## $ repo_name             <chr> "f/awesome-chatgpt-prompts", "ant-design/ant-des…
## $ repo_stars            <dbl> 122280, 94114, 86254, 77162, 42539, 40425, 31971…
## $ repo_forks            <dbl> 16401, 51745, 9485, 2995, 3047, 4297, 1269, 1713…
## $ repo_created          <dttm> 2022-12-05 13:54:13, 2015-04-24 15:37:24, 2016-…
## $ repo_updated          <dttm> 2025-03-31 14:41:25, 2025-03-30 21:31:51, 2025-…
## $ repo_description      <chr> "This repo includes ChatGPT prompt curation to u…
## $ repo_issues           <dbl> 673, 50429, 26872, 17860, 1751, 86959, 9606, 551…
## $ repo_pulls            <dbl> 673, 19344, 13608, 6546, 1138, 72015, 5814, 3848…
## $ repo_commits          <dbl> 682, 29298, 67620, 12174, 1102, 82057, 10378, 14…
## $ repo_contributors     <dbl> 124, 376, 372, 461, 104, 337, 310, 1, 420, 275, …
## $ repo_size             <dbl> 1338, 219388, 963301, 246224, 5399, 590738, 3820…
## $ repo_languages        <chr> "JavaScript: 56993; CSS: 38027; HTML: 19372; She…
## $ repo_primary_language <chr> "JavaScript", "TypeScript", "TypeScript", "Zig",…
## $ repo_age_days         <int> 1287, 4069, 3740, 1887, 895, 5767, 3096, 1548, 1…
glimpse(metrics_df)
## Rows: 806
## Columns: 5
## $ repo_name                 <chr> "hanyk/BayeSED3", "hoishing/natal", "Elevate…
## $ cursor_commits            <dbl> 2, 2, 5, 2, 6, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 10, 1, 5, 5, 1…
## $ cursor_adoptor_percentage <dbl> 100, 100, 100, 100, 100, 100, 100, 100, 100,…
## $ cursor_adoption_time      <dttm> 2024-10-05 04:51:09, 2024-12-21 23:08:54, 2…
## $ adoption_yearmonth        <chr> "2024-10", "2024-12", "2024-10", "2025-02", …

Distribution of Cursor Adoption Time

# Plot cursor adoption over time (by year-month) with bar chart and numbers on top
adoption_counts <- metrics_df %>%
    count(adoption_yearmonth) %>%
    arrange(adoption_yearmonth)

p <- ggplot(adoption_counts, aes(x = adoption_yearmonth, y = n)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#0072B2", alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
    labs(
        x = "Year-Month",
        y = "Number of Repositories"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 330), clip = "off") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(p)

ggsave("../plots/cursor_adoption_time.pdf", plot = p, width = 5, height = 2.5, device = CairoPDF, family = "DejaVu Sans")

Distribution of Repository Creation Time

# Create year-quarter field for repository creation dates
repos_df <- repos_df %>%
    mutate(
        # Extract quarter (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) from date
        quarter = paste0("Q", ceiling(month(repo_created)/3)),
        # Format as YYYY-QN
        creation_yearquarter = paste0(year(repo_created), "-", quarter)
    )

# Plot repo creation over time (by year-quarter) with bar chart and numbers on top
creation_counts <- repos_df %>%
    count(creation_yearquarter) %>%
    arrange(creation_yearquarter)

p2 <- ggplot(creation_counts, aes(x = creation_yearquarter, y = n)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#009E73", alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
    labs(
        x = "Year-Quarter",
        y = "Number of Repositories"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(creation_counts$n) * 1.1), clip = "off") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(p2)

ggsave("../plots/repo_creation_time.pdf", plot = p2, width = 9, height = 3, device = CairoPDF, family = "DejaVu Sans")

Descriptive Statistics Table in LaTeX

# Create a function to calculate descriptive statistics
calc_stats <- function(x) {
    c(
        mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
        std_dev = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE),
        min = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
        q1 = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
        q3 = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        max = max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
}

# Calculate descriptive statistics for the selected metrics
metrics <- c(
    "repo_age_days", "repo_stars", "repo_forks", "repo_contributors",
    "repo_commits", "repo_issues", "repo_pulls"
)

stats_list <- repos_df %>%
    select(all_of(metrics)) %>%
    map(calc_stats)

# Create a data frame for the table
stats_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Repository Age (days)",
    "Stars",
    "Forks",
    "Contributors",
    "Commits",
    "Issues",
    "Pull Requests"
  ),
  Mean = sapply(stats_list, function(x) round(x["mean"], 1)),
  #`Std Dev` = sapply(stats_list, function(x) round(x["std_dev"], 1)),
  Min = sapply(stats_list, function(x) round(x["min"], 1)),
  `Q1 (25%)` = sapply(stats_list, function(x) round(x["q1.25%"], 1)),
  Median = sapply(stats_list, function(x) round(x["median"], 1)),
  `Q3 (75%)` = sapply(stats_list, function(x) round(x["q3.75%"], 1)),
  Max = sapply(stats_list, function(x) round(x["max"], 1))
)

# Generate LaTeX table with fixes
table_output <- kable(stats_df, format = "latex",
      caption = "Descriptive Statistics of Repository Metrics",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      escape = FALSE)

print(table_output)
## \begin{table}
## 
## \caption{\label{tab:unnamed-chunk-5}Descriptive Statistics of Repository Metrics}
## \centering
## \begin{tabular}[t]{llrrrrrr}
## \toprule
##   & Metric & Mean & Min & Q1..25.. & Median & Q3..75.. & Max\\
## \midrule
## repo_age_days.mean & Repository Age (days) & 1163.3 & 444 & 522.5 & 682.5 & 1325.2 & 6369\\
## repo_stars.mean & Stars & 1475.3 & 10 & 20.0 & 51.0 & 242.0 & 122280\\
## repo_forks.mean & Forks & 215.9 & 0 & 3.0 & 9.0 & 37.0 & 51745\\
## repo_contributors.mean & Contributors & 19.2 & 0 & 1.0 & 3.0 & 10.0 & 461\\
## repo_commits.mean & Commits & 1816.6 & 1 & 49.0 & 209.0 & 951.8 & 86954\\
## \addlinespace
## repo_issues.mean & Issues & 1070.3 & 0 & 3.0 & 31.5 & 232.5 & 100614\\
## repo_pulls.mean & Pull Requests & 719.6 & 0 & 1.0 & 18.0 & 161.8 & 72015\\
## \bottomrule
## \end{tabular}
## \end{table}

Distribution of Repository Primary Languages

# Count repositories by primary language
language_counts <- repos_df %>%
    count(repo_primary_language) %>%
    arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    # Take top 15 languages for readability
    slice_head(n = 15)

# Plot language distribution
p <- ggplot(language_counts, aes(x = reorder(repo_primary_language, n), y = n)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.2, size = 3) +
    labs(
        x = "Primary Language",
        y = "Number of Repositories"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_flip(ylim = c(0, max(language_counts$n) * 1.1), clip = "off") +
    theme(
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
    )

print(p)

# Save the plot
ggsave("../plots/repo_language_distribution.pdf", plot = p, width = 7, height = 5)
